summary:
The best option for reducing post-dressing sensitivity involves using either a soothing mouthwash or applying a desensitizing paste aimed at addressing dentin hypersensitivity while also ensuring proper oral hygiene practices are maintained. Agent1 reasoned by focusing primarily on methods most effective against dental hypersensitivity rather than just general cleaning measures due to their specific relevance to alleviating pain from exposed tooth structures. To summarize:

To reduce post-surgery sensitivity, use a desensitizing paste like Colgate Sensitive Pro-Relief followed by gentle brushing techniques focused on keeping areas free from plaque but not overly irritated. This approach targets the primary cause—exposed dentine—and supports overall comfort recovery without introducing unnecessary risk factors such as harsh chemicals found sometimes even in strong Agent1 reasoned by focusing primarily on methods most effective against dental hypersensitivity rather than just general cleaning measures due to their specific relevance to alleviating pain from exposed tooth structures.
